L. 111–203, title XIV, § 1400(b)124 Stat. 2136July 21, 2010“Subtitles A, B, C, and E [subtitles A (§§ 1401–1406), B (§§ 1411–1422), C (§§ 1431–1433), and E (§§ 1461–1465) of title XIV of , enacting sections 1638a, 1639b to 1639d, 1639f, and 1639g of Title 15, Commerce and Trade, amending , sections 1602, 1607, 1638, 1639 to 1639d, and 1640 of Title 15, and enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 1601 and 1639b to 1639d of Title 15] and sections 1471 [enacting ], 1472 [enacting and amending ], 1475 [amending ], and 1476 [not classified to the Code], and the amendments made by such subtitles and sections, shall be enumerated consumer laws, as defined in section 1002 [], and come under the purview of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection for purposes of title X [see Short Title note set out under ], including the transfer of functions and personnel under subtitle F of title X [§§ 1061–1067, enacting part F of this subchapter] and the savings provisions of such subtitle.”Pub. children.heading: Affiliate
children.label: 1 Affiliate
children.text: The term “affiliate” means any person that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with another person.

children.heading: Bureau
children.label: 2 Bureau
children.text: The term “Bureau” means the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.

children.heading: Business of insurance
children.label: 3 Business of insurance
children.text: The term “business of insurance” means the writing of insurance or the reinsuring of risks by an insurer, including all acts necessary to such writing or reinsuring and the activities relating to the writing of insurance or the reinsuring of risks conducted by persons who act as, or are, officers, directors, agents, or employees of insurers or who are other persons authorized to act on behalf of such persons.

children.heading: Consumer
children.label: 4 Consumer
children.text: The term “consumer” means an individual or an agent, trustee, or representative acting on behalf of an individual.

children.label: A
children.text: paragraph (15) and is offered or provided for use by consumers primarily for personal, family, or household purposes; or

children.label: B
children.text: clause (i), (iii), (ix), or (x) of paragraph (15)(A), and is delivered, offered, or provided in connection with a consumer financial product or service referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: any person that engages in offering or providing a consumer financial product or service; and

children.label: B
children.text: any affiliate of a person described in subparagraph (A) if such affiliate acts as a service provider to such person.

children.heading: Credit
children.label: 7 Credit
children.text: The term “credit” means the right granted by a person to a consumer to defer payment of a debt, incur debt and defer its payment, or purchase property or services and defer payment for such purchase.

children.label: A
children.text: the acceptance of deposits, maintenance of deposit accounts, or the provision of services related to the acceptance of deposits or the maintenance of deposit accounts;

children.label: B
children.text: the acceptance of funds, the provision of other services related to the acceptance of funds, or the maintenance of member share accounts by a credit union; or

children.label: C
children.text: the receipt of funds or the equivalent thereof, as the Bureau may determine by rule or order, received or held by a covered person (or an agent for a covered person) for the purpose of facilitating a payment or transferring funds or value of funds between a consumer and a third party.

children.heading: Designated transfer date
children.label: 9 Designated transfer date
children.text: The term “designated transfer date” means the date established under .section 5582 of this title

children.heading: Director
children.label: 10 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Bureau.

children.label: A
children.text: means the provision, by a person, of electronic data transmission, routing, intermediate or transient storage, or connections to a telecommunications system or network; and

children.label: i
children.text: selects or modifies the content of the electronic data;

children.label: ii
children.text: transmits, routes, stores, or provides connections for electronic data, including financial data, in a manner that such financial data is differentiated from other types of data of the same form that such person transmits, routes, or stores, or with respect to which, provides connections; or

children.label: iii
children.text: is a payee, payor, correspondent, or similar party to a payment transaction with a consumer.

children.label: A
children.text: the Alternative Mortgage Transaction Parity Act of 1982 ( et seq.);12 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: B
children.text: the Consumer Leasing Act of 1976 ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 1667

children.label: C
children.text: the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( et seq.), except with respect to section 920 of that Act [–2];15 U.S.C. 169315 U.S.C. 1693o

children.label: D
children.text: the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 1691

children.label: E
children.text: the Fair Credit Billing Act ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 1666

children.label: F
children.text: the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( et seq.), except with respect to sections 615(e) and 628 of that Act (, 1681w);15 U.S.C. 168115 U.S.C. 1681m(e)

children.label: G
children.text: the Home Owners Protection Act of 1998 ( et seq.);212 U.S.C. 4901So in original. Probably should be “Homeowners”.2

children.label: H
children.text: the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 1692

children.label: I
children.text: subsections (b) through (f) of section 43 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act ([(b)]–(f));12 U.S.C. 1831t(c)

children.label: J
children.text: sections 502 through 509 of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (9) except for section 505 [] as it applies to section 501(b) [];15 U.S.C. 6802–68015 U.S.C. 680515 U.S.C. 6801(b)

children.label: K
children.text: the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act of 1975 ( et seq.);12 U.S.C. 2801

children.label: L
children.text: the Home Ownership and Equity Protection Act of 1994 ( note);15 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: M
children.text: the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 ( et seq.);12 U.S.C. 2601

children.label: N
children.text: the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 ( et seq.);12 U.S.C. 5101

children.label: O
children.text: the Truth in Lending Act ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: P
children.text: the Truth in Savings Act ( et seq.);12 U.S.C. 4301

children.label: Q
children.text: section 626 of the Omnibus Appropriations Act, 2009 () []; andPublic Law 111–812 U.S.C. 5538

children.label: R
children.text: the Interstate Land Sales Full Disclosure Act ().15 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Fair lending
children.label: 13 Fair lending
children.text: The term “fair lending” means fair, equitable, and nondiscriminatory access to credit for consumers.

children.heading: Federal consumer financial law
children.label: 14 Federal consumer financial law
children.text: The term “Federal consumer financial law” means the provisions of this title, the enumerated consumer laws, the laws for which authorities are transferred under subtitles F and H, and any rule or order prescribed by the Bureau under this title, an enumerated consumer law, or pursuant to the authorities transferred under subtitles F and H. The term does not include the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.].1115 U.S.C. 41

children.label: i
children.text: extending credit and servicing loans, including acquiring, purchasing, selling, brokering, or other extensions of credit (other than solely extending commercial credit to a person who originates consumer credit transactions);

children.label: I
children.text: the lease is on a non-operating basis;

children.label: II
children.text: the initial term of the lease is at least 90 days; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a lease involving real property, at the inception of the initial lease, the transaction is intended to result in ownership of the leased property to be transferred to the lessee, subject to standards prescribed by the Bureau;

children.label: iii
children.text: providing real estate settlement services, except such services excluded under subparagraph (C), or performing appraisals of real estate or personal property;

children.label: iv
children.text: engaging in deposit-taking activities, transmitting or exchanging funds, or otherwise acting as a custodian of funds or any financial instrument for use by or on behalf of a consumer;

children.label: I
children.text: a seller shall not be found to exercise substantial control over the terms or conditions of the stored value if the seller is not a party to the contract with the consumer for the stored value product, and another person is principally responsible for establishing the terms or conditions of the stored value; and

children.label: II
children.text: advertising the nonfinancial goods or services of the seller on the stored value card or device is not in itself an exercise of substantial control over the terms or conditions;

children.label: vi
children.text: providing check cashing, check collection, or check guaranty services;

children.label: I
children.text: is a merchant, retailer, or seller of any nonfinancial good or service who engages in financial data processing by transmitting or storing payments data about a consumer exclusively for purpose of initiating payments instructions by the consumer to pay such person for the purchase of, or to complete a commercial transaction for, such nonfinancial good or service sold directly by such person to the consumer; or

children.label: II
children.text: provides access to a host server to a person for purposes of enabling that person to establish and maintain a website;

children.label: I
children.text: providing credit counseling to any consumer; and

children.label: II
children.text: providing services to assist a consumer with debt management or debt settlement, modifying the terms of any extension of credit, or avoiding foreclosure;

children.label: aa
children.text: collects, analyzes, or maintains information that relates solely to the transactions between a consumer and such person;

children.label: bb
children.text: provides the information described in item (aa) to an affiliate of such person; or

children.label: cc
children.text: provides information that is used or expected to be used solely in any decision regarding the offering or provision of a product or service that is not a consumer financial product or service, including a decision for employment, government licensing, or a residential lease or tenancy involving a consumer; and

children.label: II
children.text: the information described in subclause (I)(aa) is not used by such person or affiliate in connection with any decision regarding the offering or provision of a consumer financial product or service to the consumer, other than credit described in ;section 5517(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: x
children.text: collecting debt related to any consumer financial product or service; and

children.label: I
children.text: entered into or conducted as a subterfuge or with a purpose to evade any Federal consumer financial law; or

children.label: II
children.text: permissible for a bank or for a financial holding company to offer or to provide under any provision of a Federal law or regulation applicable to a bank or a financial holding company, and has, or likely will have, a material impact on consumers.

children.label: I
children.text: Providing information products or services to a covered person for identity authentication.

children.label: II
children.text: Providing information products or services for fraud or identify theft detection, prevention, or investigation.

children.label: III
children.text: Providing document retrieval or delivery services.

children.label: IV
children.text: Providing public records information retrieval.

children.label: V
children.text: Providing information products or services for anti-money laundering activities.

children.label: I
children.text: examination or enforcement powers authority under this title with respect to a covered person or service provider engaging in an activity described in subparagraph (A)(ix); or1

children.label: II
children.text: powers authorized by this title to prescribe rules, issue orders, or take other actions under any enumerated consumer law or law for which the authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.1

children.label: i
children.text: the business of insurance; or

children.label: ii
children.text: electronic conduit services.

children.heading: Foreign exchange
children.label: 16 Foreign exchange
children.text: The term “foreign exchange” means the exchange, for compensation, of currency of the United States or of a foreign government for currency of another government.

children.heading: Insured credit union
children.label: 17 Insured credit union
children.text: The term “insured credit union” has the same meaning as in .section 1752 of this title

children.heading: Payment instrument
children.label: 18 Payment instrument
children.text: The term “payment instrument” means a check, draft, warrant, money order, traveler’s check, electronic instrument, or other instrument, payment of funds, or monetary value (other than currency).

children.heading: Person
children.label: 19 Person
children.text: The term “person” means an individual, partnership, company, corporation, association (incorporated or unincorporated), trust, estate, cooperative organization, or other entity.

children.heading: Person regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
children.label: 20 Person regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
children.text: The term “person regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission” means any person that is registered, or required by statute or regulation to be registered, with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, but only to the extent that the activities of such person are subject to the jurisdiction of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission under the Commodity Exchange Act [ et seq.].7 U.S.C. 1

children.label: A
children.text: a broker or dealer that is required to be registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [ et seq.];15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: B
children.text: an investment adviser that is registered under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 [ et seq.];15 U.S.C. 80b–1

children.label: C
children.text: an investment company that is required to be registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 [ et seq.], and any company that has elected to be regulated as a business development company under that Act;15 U.S.C. 80a–1

children.label: D
children.text: a national securities exchange that is required to be registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934;

children.label: E
children.text: a transfer agent that is required to be registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934;

children.label: F
children.text: a clearing corporation that is required to be registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934;

children.label: G
children.text: any self-regulatory organization that is required to be registered with the Commission;

children.label: H
children.text: any nationally recognized statistical rating organization that is required to be registered with the Commission;

children.label: I
children.text: any securities information processor that is required to be registered with the Commission;

children.label: J
children.text: any municipal securities dealer that is required to be registered with the Commission;

children.label: K
children.text: any other person that is required to be registered with the Commission under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934; and

children.label: L
children.text: any employee, agent, or contractor acting on behalf of, registered with, or providing services to, any person described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (K), but only to the extent that any person described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (K), or the employee, agent, or contractor of such person, acts in a regulated capacity.

children.heading: Person regulated by a State insurance regulator
children.label: 22 Person regulated by a State insurance regulator
children.text: The term “person regulated by a State insurance regulator” means any person that is engaged in the business of insurance and subject to regulation by any State insurance regulator, but only to the extent that such person acts in such capacity.

children.heading: Person that performs income tax preparation activities for consumers
children.label: 23 Person that performs income tax preparation activities for consumers
A
children.text: any tax return preparer (as defined in ), regardless of whether compensated, but only to the extent that the person acts in such capacity;section 7701(a)(36) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: any person regulated by the Secretary under , but only to the extent that the person acts in such capacity; andsection 330 of title 31

children.label: C
children.text: any authorized IRS e-file Providers (as defined for purposes of ), but only to the extent that the person acts in such capacity.section 7216 of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an insured depository institution or depository institution holding company (as defined in ), or subsidiary of such institution or company, the appropriate Federal banking agency, as that term is defined in ; andsection 1813 of this titlesection 1813 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an insured credit union, the National Credit Union Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: shall apply only with respect to a covered person that is not a bank holding company (as that term is defined in ), credit union, or depository institution;section 1841 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall be deemed to mean a covered person for all purposes of any provision of Federal consumer financial law; and

children.label: i
children.text: any director, officer, or employee charged with managerial responsibility for, or controlling shareholder of, or agent for, such covered person;

children.label: ii
children.text: any shareholder, consultant, joint venture partner, or other person, as determined by the Bureau (by rule or on a case-by-case basis) who materially participates in the conduct of the affairs of such covered person; and

children.label: I
children.text: violation of any provision of law or regulation; or

children.label: II
children.text: breach of a fiduciary duty.

children.label: i
children.text: participates in designing, operating, or maintaining the consumer financial product or service; or

children.label: ii
children.text: processes transactions relating to the consumer financial product or service (other than unknowingly or incidentally transmitting or processing financial data in a manner that such data is undifferentiated from other types of data of the same form as the person transmits or processes).

children.label: i
children.text: a support service of a type provided to businesses generally or a similar ministerial service; or

children.label: ii
children.text: time or space for an advertisement for a consumer financial product or service through print, newspaper, or electronic media.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: A person that is a service provider shall be deemed to be a covered person to the extent that such person engages in the offering or provision of its own consumer financial product or service.

children.heading: State
children.label: 27 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State, territory, or possession of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, American Samoa, or the United States Virgin Islands or any federally recognized Indian tribe, as defined by the Secretary of the Interior under .section 5131(a) of title 25

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “stored value” means funds or monetary value represented in any electronic format, whether or not specially encrypted, and stored or capable of storage on electronic media in such a way as to be retrievable and transferred electronically, and includes a prepaid debit card or product, or any other similar product, regardless of whether the amount of the funds or monetary value may be increased or reloaded.

children.label: i
children.text: issued by a merchant, retailer, or other seller of nonfinancial goods or services;

children.label: ii
children.text: redeemable only for transactions with the merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services or with an affiliate of such person, which affiliate itself is a merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services;

children.label: iii
children.text: issued in a specified amount that, except in the case of a card or product used solely for telephone services, may not be increased or reloaded;

children.label: iv
children.text: purchased on a prepaid basis in exchange for payment; and

children.label: v
children.text: honored upon presentation to such merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services or an affiliate of such person, which affiliate itself is a merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services, only for any nonfinancial goods or services.

children.heading: Transmitting or exchanging funds
children.label: 29 Transmitting or exchanging funds
children.text: The term “transmitting or exchanging funds” means receiving currency, monetary value, or payment instruments from a consumer for the purpose of exchanging or transmitting the same by any means, including transmission by wire, facsimile, electronic transfer, courier, the Internet, or through bill payment services or through other businesses that facilitate third-party transfers within the United States or to or from the United States.
